name: libregexp-wildcards-perl
title: libregexp-wildcards-perl
summary: converts wildcard expressions to Perl regular expressions
description:
converts wildcard expressions to Perl regular expressions
In many situations, users may want to specify patterns to match but
don't need the full power of regexps. Wildcards make one of those sets
of simplified rules. Regexp::Wildcards converts wildcard expressions
to Perl regular expressions, so that you can use them for matching.
.
It handles the * and ? wildcards, as well as Unix bracketed
alternatives {,}, but also % and _ SQL wildcards. If required, it can
also keep original (...) groups or ^ and $ anchors. Backslash (\) is
used as an escape character.
.
Typesets that mimic the behaviour of Windows and Unix shells are also
provided.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libregexp-wildcards-perl because converts wildcard expressions to Perl regular expressions.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libregexp-wildcards-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libregexp-wildcards-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libregexp-wildcards-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
